page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. The CPU includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptography but lacking x86-specific vector extensions like AVX. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks place the c8gn.xlarge in the average tier for most operations. Single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests yield scores of 1707.39 and 6817.26 respectively. The server demonstrates strong performance in single-threaded compression and decompression, with gzip compression reaching 30.32 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.43 GB/sec. However, cryptographic performance is a notable bottleneck, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the poor tier at 750.19. Memory bandwidth is robust, measuring 462.19 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 96.29 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

Network performance is a key feature, offering a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the arm64 architecture and dedicated core allocation deliver a balanced resource density for compute-bound workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. The instance is well-suited for network-optimized applications, static web serving, Redis key-value databases processing up to 9.4 million SET operations per second, and software compilation tasks. It is less suitable for heavy cryptographic workloads or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 4 dedicated physical cores, 8.0 GB of memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU. Benchmarks show strong performance in single-threaded compression tasks, such as gzip at 30.32 GB/sec, and average results in multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. However, cryptographic performance is relatively weak. This instance is highly efficient for network-bound workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and Redis database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 8 GB RAM, 12 Gbps network bandwidth, and strong single-threaded compression performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 8 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and high-speed networking for efficient web serving and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 processor with 4 dedicated cores
8.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for data transfer
Strong single-threaded compression and decompression performance
Suited for static web serving, Redis databases, and software compilation
